metrics.narrative: Simpson articulated qualitative pain around invoicing inefficiency and intake process gaps on the 30 April and 8 May discovery calls, but no financial impact, headcount burden, cycle-time cost, error rate, or ROI target was quantified. Suzanne Mallette (Sourcing) described current workarounds ('we handle invoicing manually and intake is ad hoc') without naming the cost of those workarounds. Vincent Jiang (IT) noted integration concerns but no system-cost baseline. With 82 days to close and no quantified business case, there is no financial justification to accelerate the timeline or secure executive sponsorship. The highest-leverage move is to run a joint scoping session with Sourcing and Finance to quantify invoice-processing cost, intake-request cycle time, and target ROI, then anchor the business case to a named financial owner (CFO or VP Finance) by mid-July.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Suzanne Mallette (Sourcing) led the 30 April and 8 May discovery calls and asked substantive questions (taxonomy support, intake functionality), signaling interest but not advocacy. There is no evidence she has introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, driven the agenda, pushed back on competitors, or shared internal Simpson docs. Her engagement is cooling: last contact 8 May (57 days ago), no reply to the 27 May rep follow-up ('Follow up with Suzette'), and no OpportunityContactRole designation. Edgar Karssenberg, Alex Bouterse, and Vincent Jiang (IT) attended the 8 May call but have not re-engaged. No Champion or Coach contact role is populated. The deal is single-threaded to a Sourcing practitioner with no executive access, no multi-threading beyond IT, and no active advocacy. The evidence shows early-stage technical interest that has stalled, not championship. The move is to re-engage Suzanne, assess her current stance and access to the EB, and if she cannot or will not champion, identify an alternative advocate (VP Strategic Sourcing is an account contact but not engaged) or escalate via the deal owner's manager to the CFO to build top-down pull by mid-July.

sources: Avoma discovery calls 30 Apr and 8 May: no external competitor named, current state described as manual/ad hoc
SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Moved_To__c: all null
57-day silence after 8 May: unknown if paused, deprioritized, or running silent RFP
competition.narrative: No external competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) was named on the discovery calls or logged in Salesforce (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Moved_To__c all null). Suzanne Mallette described the current state as 'we handle invoicing manually and intake is ad hoc,' which positions the primary competition as Do Nothing / Current Workaround (manual processes, no automation). This is a common early-stage read in large enterprise deals where the buyer has not yet committed to solving the pain or allocated budget, making inertia the real competitor. However, the 57-day silence and lack of follow-up raise the possibility that Simpson has quietly shifted to another priority, paused the evaluation, or is running a silent multi-vendor RFP without disclosing it to the relevant stakeholder, RFI, or vendor-selection mention appeared in calls or tasks. The reconciled read: confirmed competition is Do Nothing (inertia, manual workarounds), with unknown risk of undisclosed external competitors or deprioritization. The wedge against Do Nothing is to quantify the cost of manual invoicing and ad hoc intake (error rate, cycle time, FTE burden) and tie it to a compelling event (fiscal-year planning, ERP upgrade, audit finding), then position Merlin Intake + ANA as the step-change automation that justifies the investment. The move is to confirm in re-engagement whether Simpson is evaluating other vendors or has paused, and if still active, run the quantified business case by late July.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are undefined. An NDA was requested by Zycus on 3 May (per Next_Step__c) and is now 8 weeks overdue with no completion logged in tasks, emails, or calls. No MSA, SOW, or legal-review discussion has occurred. InfoSec requirements, procurement channel (direct vs three-way via SI/partner), and ERP/systems-integration scoping are unaddressed. Vincent Jiang raised Oracle ERP integration on 8 May but no follow-on technical deep-dive or API scoping session was scheduled. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so no incumbent-contract forcing function exists. With 82 days to close, a typical 4-6 week legal/InfoSec/redlining cycle plus 2-3 weeks for procurement and SOW finalization cannot fit unless contracting starts immediately, but the NDA (the first gate) remains unsigned. The evidence shows contracting has not started and the buyer has not prioritized it. The move is to re-engage, confirm active intent, fast-track the NDA, and if intent is real, run a joint contracting-timeline workshop with the relevant stakeholder and Procurement by late July to map the critical path.
